name: Sierra Leone
summary: Sierra Leone is the strongest data-exchange demo: the opportunity is to connect public finance, procurement, civil registration, and banking systems into visible service outcomes.

leadAgencies.name: MoCTI / DSTI
leadAgencies.role: Digital transformation lead, interoperability governance, and technical safeguards.

leadAgencies.name: Ministry of Finance
leadAgencies.role: Fiscal backbone, recurrent-cost model, IFMIS, and sustainability anchor.

leadAgencies.name: NPPA
leadAgencies.role: Procurement data, supplier records, contract awards, and eGP operating model.

leadAgencies.name: Bank of Sierra Leone
leadAgencies.role: Banking settlement and payment status through T24 or related rails.

systems.name: Government Service Bus / X-Road 7.7.1 environments
systems.note: Secure data exchange layer connecting approved agencies through service contracts.

systems.name: CRVS / NCRA
systems.note: Use minimal identity validation tokens rather than copying raw identity data.

systems.name: eGP / NPPA
systems.note: Electronic procurement, supplier, award, and contract records.

systems.name: IFMIS
systems.note: Treasury approval, budget execution, and payment instruction.

systems.name: Temenos T24 / Bank settlement
systems.note: Settlement confirmation and payment status for supplier payments.

systems.name: Feed Salone farmer registry
systems.note: Farmer profiles, farm and plot data, extension records, subsidy targeting, and grievance workflows.

openQuestions: What supplier payment status can be made visible?
How will farmers correct or appeal registry errors?

name: Ethiopia
summary: Ethiopia is the clearest example of DPI as a live government service platform: MESOB combines service delivery, Fayda identity, API exchange, branch networks, and a future sovereign payment layer.

leadAgencies.name: PMO / Ministry of Finance
leadAgencies.role: Political mandate, fiscal accountability, and programme sign-off.

leadAgencies.name: Civil Service Commission
leadAgencies.role: Service-delivery modernization and ministry process alignment.

leadAgencies.name: National ID stakeholders
leadAgencies.role: Fayda authentication, identity verification, and inclusion safeguards.

leadAgencies.name: National Bank of Ethiopia
leadAgencies.role: Oversight for MESOB Pay, wallet roadmap, and payment regulation.

systems.name: MESOB one-stop service platform
systems.note: Citizen-facing service gateway and service orchestration layer.

systems.name: Fayda Digital ID
systems.note: Authentication and remote identity verification for service users.

systems.name: APISIX integration gateway
systems.note: API routing, authorization, audit logging, and ministry data access.

systems.name: MESOB Pay / wallet roadmap
systems.note: Future sovereign payment gateway or wallet for public service flows.

systems.name: Community Service Centers
systems.note: Assisted access points for people who cannot use digital channels alone.

systems.name: OpenAgriNet
systems.note: Agriculture records and service integration opportunity.

openQuestions: Which services should be first through MESOB?
What data can ministries expose through the gateway?
How will assisted access and exception handling work?

name: Zambia
summary: Zambia is the Cohort 1 example for land, climate, and natural-resource governance: the opportunity is to connect ZILAS, forest traceability, community forest records, and public-service rails.

leadAgencies.name: Smart Zambia Institute
leadAgencies.role: Central digital convener and Government Service Bus steward.

leadAgencies.name: Land administration stakeholders
leadAgencies.role: ZILAS, title data, customary land workflows, and land-service design.

leadAgencies.name: Forestry and natural-resource agencies
leadAgencies.role: Timber traceability, forest permits, community forest records, and provenance.

leadAgencies.name: Ministry of Health ecosystem
leadAgencies.role: Smart Care Pro, DHIS2, eLMIS, patient records, and medicine accountability.

systems.name: Smart Zambia Government Service Bus
systems.note: Service bus pattern for land, health, finance, identity, and other agency exchange.

systems.name: ZILAS land administration
systems.note: Land administration records, title data, and customary land workflows.

systems.name: Timber Traceability System
systems.note: Permit, provenance, revenue, and forest-product traceability records.

systems.name: Community Forest Management Group records
systems.note: Community forest stewardship, eligibility, and reporting records.

systems.name: Smart Care Pro / DHIS2 / eLMIS
systems.note: Patient, facility, medicine, and health-reporting systems.

systems.name: MTN, Airtel, Zamtel and bank rails
systems.note: Payment and reconciliation rails for public-service flows.

openQuestions: How will customary land records be represented?
What forest provenance data can be shared safely?
How will identity, health, and payment data be separated?
